title: Mini baked cheesecakes
subtitle: Individual New York-style cheesecakes in a muffin tin
excerpt: Individual baked cheesecakes on a digestive biscuit base, New York-style: dense, smooth, and vanilla-forward. Made in a muffin tin and topped with a berry or a spoonful of soured cream.
sourceNotes: New York-style baked cheesecake is defined by its use of cream cheese (specifically Philadelphia, since the 1870s) and a dense, barely-set filling achieved through low-temperature baking. The individual muffin-tin format became popular in American home baking from the 1990s onward as a portion-controlled alternative to the full cheesecake. This method draws on the ratio documented in Dorie Greenspan's Baking from My Home to Yours (2006) adapted for the smaller tin format.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best made the day before. Refrigerate overnight and top on the day of serving.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; low temperature prevents cracking
baking.decoratingTechnique: fresh berries or a spoonful of soured cream on top of each chilled cheesecake

content.text: Individual cheesecakes bake faster and more evenly than a single large cheesecake. The muffin tin format also means they unmould cleanly as individual servings with no risk of a collapsed slice. The low oven temperature is intentional: it prevents the top from cracking and keeps the filling smooth.

All the filling ingredients must be at room temperature. Cold cream cheese beaten into a batter will leave lumps that do not smooth out during baking. Take the cream cheese and eggs out of the fridge at least 1 hour before you begin.

Heat the oven to 150°C fan. Line a 12-hole muffin tin with paper cases. Mix the crushed {{digestive-biscuits}} with the melted {{unsalted-butter}}. Press 1 heaped tablespoon of biscuit mixture into each case and press down firmly. Beat the {{cream-cheese}} and {{caster-sugar}} until smooth. Beat in the {{eggs}} one at a time, then the {{double-cream}}, {{vanilla-extract}}, and {{lemon-juice}} until the filling is smooth and pourable.

Divide the filling evenly over the biscuit bases. Bake for 22 to 25 minutes until the edges are set and the centres still have a slight wobble. Turn the oven off and leave the cheesecakes inside with the door ajar for 20 minutes. This prevents cracking by reducing the temperature change. Transfer to a rack to cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 4 hours before removing from the cases and topping.
